narrative.trigger_event: Sovereign debt crisis re-emerges in peripheral EUR economies amid fiscal deterioration and political fragmentation
transmission_channels: Peripheral sovereign spreads widen sharply (Italy, Spain, Portugal)
Sovereign-bank doom loop re-emerges as banks hold government debt
EUR depreciates on capital flight and ECB credibility concerns
Credit spreads widen, especially for EUR financials
Flight to quality into German Bunds and USD

timeline.onset: Q2 2025 - Sovereign debt auction fails, spreads spike
timeline.peak: Q3 2025 - Banking sector stress, EUR weakness accelerates
timeline.recovery: Q4 2025-Q1 2026 - ECB intervention, fiscal support package

key_assumptions: ECB constrained by inflation, cannot immediately ease
Fiscal rules prevent aggressive national responses
Political will for bailouts limited vs 2011-2012
Contagion contained to EUR (limited global spillover)
narrative.narrative_text:
**Trigger Event**: Sovereign debt crisis re-emerges in peripheral EUR economies amid fiscal deterioration and political fragmentation

**Scenario Type**: Recession
**Severity**: Severe Stress

**Transmission Channels**:
1. Peripheral sovereign spreads widen sharply (Italy, Spain, Portugal)
2. Sovereign-bank doom loop re-emerges as banks hold government debt
3. EUR depreciates on capital flight and ECB credibility concerns
4. Credit spreads widen, especially for EUR financials
5. Flight to quality into German Bunds and USD

**Timeline**:
- **Onset**: Q2 2025 - Sovereign debt auction fails, spreads spike
- **Peak**: Q3 2025 - Banking sector stress, EUR weakness accelerates
- **Recovery**: Q4 2025-Q1 2026 - ECB intervention, fiscal support package

asset_class_shocks.description: Interest rate curve shocks (bps) and FX movements (% change in value of 1 USD)
asset_class_shocks.rationale: Flight to quality drives DM rates down; EM rates up on risk premium. USD strengthens as safe haven.

asset_class_shocks.description: Credit spread relative moves by sector and region with beta multipliers
asset_class_shocks.rationale: Recession scenario drives credit risk repricing. Spreads widen due to default risk and liquidity premium. High yield particularly affected. Cyclical sectors (Energy, Materials) see elevated betas.

asset_class_shocks.description: Energy commodity shocks as relative price moves and absolute volatility shocks
asset_class_shocks.rationale: Demand destruction from economic contraction drives oil prices lower. OPEC+ production cuts insufficient to offset demand weakness.

asset_class_shocks.description: Precious metals shocks: relative price, relative volatility, absolute lease rate (bps)
asset_class_shocks.rationale: Gold and silver benefit from safe haven demand in risk-off scenario. Industrial PMs (Platinum, Palladium) decline with broader industrial demand weakness. Lease rates fall reflecting lower cost of carry.

asset_class_shocks.description: Base metals shocks: relative price and absolute volatility
asset_class_shocks.rationale: Industrial metals decline on weaker global growth expectations. Copper acts as economic bellwether. Volatility increases reflecting uncertainty.

validation_results.message: Missing asset classes: rates, fx, credit, commodities
validation_results.suggestion: Complete scenarios should cover all major asset classes

Energy Trading: Review EUR Sovereign Crisis 2.0 oil price shocks vs current positioning
Confirm relevance to key trading strategies (calendar spreads, crack spreads)
Identify any basis risks (Brent-WTI, physical vs futures) needing explicit shocks
Validate vol shock magnitudes vs current market conditions
Precious Metals: Review precious metals shocks vs inventory positions
Confirm lease rate assumptions align with financing strategies
Identify any cross-commodity risks not captured
Base Metals: Validate base metals shocks vs key positions (especially copper)
Confirm calendar spread impacts captured (pending Murex migration)
Review any specific contract types (LME, SHFE) needing separate shocks
Credit Trading: Review sector beta assumptions vs current portfolio composition
Identify any specific issuer concentrations requiring attention
Confirm alignment with issuer risk exposures

next_steps: ⚠️  Review validation warnings with Market Risk SME
Conduct Front Office consultation (use prompts provided)
Present to Market Risk team for technical review
